violations.text: Thermometers are lacking in the (all ) refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

(2 Dr Reach in ) located in the (kitchen ) area was not in good repair. Ambient temperatures observed at 49DF. Establishment not allowed to store any TCS food product in unit until repaired and/or replaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/21/2025

(Empanadas) was observed held at (119 ) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC reheated food product to 165 or above. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/15/2025
Inspector observed a rodent burrow located in outside dumpster area. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

(2 dr reach in and serving line cold hold unit ) located in the (kitchen and front counter ) area was not in good repair.Ambient temperatures observed at 50df and 46DF. Establishment not allowed to store any TCS product in unit until repaired and/or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
The condensate from the walk in cooler evaporator pan is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the (basement) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025

The floors and walls throughout the basement have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/28/2025
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

Ground beef, pasta, etc. were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025
Inspector observed rodenticide (green disks) being used within the establishment. Owner informed to cease practice. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

Per PIC, grease trap is in and observed in poor repair in the basement area. Equipment components such as doors, seals, hinges, fasteners, and kick plates shall be kept intact, tight, and adjusted in accordance with manufacturer's specifications. Unit is to be serviced/repaired and in good working order. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025
Inspector observed liquid waste in basement. PIC instructed employee to clean and sanitize entire area during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

The dumpster and surrounding area were observed overflowing with trash and unnecessary items. PIC called for cleaning of area and contacted dumpster Co. during inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/30/2025

Raw animal foods (raw chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (red peppers) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

(Chicken) was observed held at (107) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

The presence of (flies and rodents ) is not controlled as evidenced by (live activity of flies and droppings in basement). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025
(Food product) were observed stored on the floor in the (walk in cooler ). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the (food storage shelves in basement ) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and rodent droppings. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025
Outside area was observed with an accumulation of trash ,litter, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

The floors in basement have an accumulation of soil residue, food debris, and pooled. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean.Grease trap in the basement was observed being cleaned during inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the (basement ) area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 07/14/2025

The presence of flies are not controlled as evidenced by live activity. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
The refuse has an accumulation of litter and unnecessary item(s). PIC informed to clean and maintain. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2025
Areas of basement observed with soil residue and pieces of insulation. PIC informed to thoroughly clean, sanitize and maintain. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
Nesting material and rub marks observed in several areas of the basement. Owner informed to clean and monitor areas. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
Inspector observed clutter and unused items in outside area(s). Owner informed to remove all unecessary items. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025
The hand-washing sinks in the kitchen and serving area were lacking paper towels. Paper towel and napkins provided. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025
Inspector observed a bag of carrots slimy with visible white debris. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as bread) in the two door lo boy. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated eggs during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

The presence of rodents are not controlled as evidenced by rub marks, nesting material, gnawing at door and electrical wire, burrows and droppings throughout food prep area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 01/27/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the grease trap, trays and prep tables throughout the food prep area have an accumulation of rodent droppings, dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. All surfaces to be thoroughly cleaned and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The dumpster area and area outside of side door has an accumulation of trash, debris and unused equipment (fryolator, wiring, shopping carts, etc). Area to be cleaned and maintained. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

Several areas in the food prep areas and basement are in poor repair. Several openings observed. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair and seal to prevent entry of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025

The entire establishment (kitchen, walk-in cooler, food prep areas and basement) have an accumulation of rodent droppings, soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2025
The hand-washing sink in the front counter area was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

(Various empanadas and rice) was observed held at (102- 131) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC reheated to 165 during inspection Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024

(Macaroni Salad) was observed held at (52) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC moved to colder unit Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024
Outside refuse area observed with a small accumulation of trash, litter, and/or other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/29/2024
The hand-washing sink in the service area was lacking soap and paper towels. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024

Recently prepared potato salad and pasta salad was observed held at 47 and 49degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC place in refrigeration to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024

The door to the outside, located in the dry storeroom, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 02/09/2024

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by dirty knives. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Knives were moved to 3-bay sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen and the basement were lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024
The front service area is lacking a hand-washing sink. Inspector observed adequate plumbing for hand-washing sink installation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024
Inspector observed various desserts from an unapproved source. PIC informed to cease sale of products and to remove from establishment. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024

Sliced tomatoes were observed at 52-degrees Fahrenheit in the service prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Containers were moved to refrigeration for rapid cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. A large, covered plastic container of beans prepared this morning was observed with a temperature of 80. Staff placed beans in shallow metal pans to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024

The door to the outside, located in the dry storeroom, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/20/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by large rodent droppings in basement. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024

Inspector observed a live cat in the kitchen during time of inspection. Feline feces and empty cat food cans were observed in basement. Staff removed cat during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024
Multiple food items were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in refrigerator and freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024
Cleaned and sanitized equipment and utensils are not stored inverted. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the front service area. Inspector observed packages of single use containers stored on the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 02/06/2024
